import pytest

def is_balanced_parens(s: str) -> bool:
    stack = []
    pairs = {'(': ')', '[': ']', '{': '}'}
    for c in s:
        if c in pairs:
            stack.append(pairs[c])
        elif c in pairs.values():
            if not stack or stack.pop() != c:
                return False
    return len(stack) == 0

class TestIsBalancedParens:
    
    # Happy path tests
    def test_empty_string(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("") == True
    
    def test_single_pair_parentheses(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("()") == True
    
    def test_single_pair_brackets(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("[]") == True
    
    def test_single_pair_braces(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("{}") == True
    
    def test_multiple_pairs_same_type(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("()()()") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("[[][]]") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("{{}{}}") == True
    
    def test_nested_pairs(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("(()())") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("((()))") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("[({})]") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("{[()]}") == True
    
    def test_mixed_balanced_pairs(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("()[]{}") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("([{}])") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("{[()()]}") == True
    
    def test_complex_balanced_expression(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("(([]){})") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("({[]})") == True
    
    # Edge cases
    def test_only_opening_brackets(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("(") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("[") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("{") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("(((") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("([{" ) == False
    
    def test_only_closing_brackets(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens(")") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("]") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("}") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens(")))") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens(")]}") == False
    
    def test_mismatched_pairs(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("(]") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("([)]") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("{[}]") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("(((") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens(")))") == False
    
    def test_wrong_order(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens(")(") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("][") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("}{") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens(")(()" == False
    
    def test_unbalanced_nested(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("((())") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("(()))") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("{[}]" ) == False
    
    # Error cases and special scenarios
    def test_with_other_characters(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("hello(world)") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("a(b]c") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("(hello[world])") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("func(arg1, arg2)") == True
    
    def test_with_whitespace(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens(" ( ) ") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens(" ( ] ") == False
        assert is_balanced_parens("\n\t() ") == True
    
    def test_with_numbers_and_symbols(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("1(2[3]4)5") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("x = [1, 2, 3]") == True
        assert is_balanced_parens("if (x > 0)
